data.title: Jiuzi New Energy's board of directors approves up to $1 billion in crypto asset investments
data.description: Jiuzi New Energy, a US-listed company, announced that its board of directors has officially approved and adopted a crypto asset investment policy, authorizing the company to deploy up to $1 billion to purchase crypto assets to ensure manageable risk. The policy authorizes Jiuzi New Energy to allocate a portion of its cash reserves to select crypto assets within a prudent risk management framework. Initial investments will be limited to BTC, ETH, and BNB. Any future expansion into other assets will require reassessment and approval by the board's risk committee. (PR Newswire)

data.title: Charles Schwab CEO: I am indeed concerned about young investors interested in cryptocurrencies
data.description: The CEO of Charles Schwab expressed concern about young investors interested in cryptocurrencies. (Zhitong Finance) Schwab previously released the results of its Q3 2025 Trader Sentiment Survey, which showed a significant rebound in market optimism. Among traders, 43% were bullish on cryptocurrencies, 38% on Bitcoin options ETFs, and 32% on spot cryptocurrency ETFs, all higher than in Q2 2025.

data.title: Native Markets: Over $15 million USDH minted in the past 24 hours
data.description: Hyperliquid's stablecoin, USDH, has officially launched after Native Markets won the bid. Native Markets announced on the X platform that USDH is now available to all Hyperliquid users. The USDH/USDC spot order book is now open on HyperCore. Over $15 million in USDH has been minted in the past 24 hours.

data.title: DeFi Technologies' Valour launches 13 cryptocurrency ETPs including HYPE
data.description: Valour, a subsidiary of Nasdaq-listed DeFi Technologies, announced the launch of 13 cryptocurrency ETPs denominated in Swedish krona on the Swedish Spotlight Stock Market. These include PEPE (PEPE), Flare (FLR), Virtuals (VIRTUAL), Optimism (OP), Story (IP), Immutable (IMX), Quant (QNT), The Graph (GRT), Floki (FLOKI), Theta (THETA), Four (FORM), IOTA (IOTA), and Hyperliquid (HYPE), bringing the total number of its digital asset ETP products to 99. (Globenewswire)

data.title: K33: The derivatives market reflects defensiveness and remains cautious about the recent trend of BTC
data.description: K33 pointed out in its analysis report: Monday's plunge caused the evaporation of $285 million in long positions and triggered $1.6 billion in liquidations across the entire cryptocurrency market. This plunge was the fourth largest liquidation event for Bitcoin in 2025 and the highest single-day liquidation of digital assets since February. In addition, BTC's seven-day actual volatility fell to just 0.6% last week, the lowest level since August 2023. Once positions are overly tilted, sudden chaos may occur. K 33 remains cautious about the near-term outlook, and the broader derivatives market reflects the same defensiveness. Chicago Mercantile Exchange (CME) futures trading remains sluggish, with the September contract once falling below the spot price. (The Block)

data.title: Hyperliquid’s USDH stablecoin officially launched after winning the bid on Native Markets
data.description: Native Markets' USDH stablecoin has launched on the Hyperliquid platform, with the USDH/USDC trading pair now open. Initial trading volume indicates a cautious but active start to the market, reaching approximately $2.2 million as the market launches. USDH is the first dollar-pegged token issued through Hyperliquid's validator selection process. It is a stablecoin natively issued on the HyperEVM and can be traded across the Hyperliquid ecosystem. Native Markets won the bid earlier this month, beating proposals from several companies, including Paxos, Frax, and Agora. (The Block)

data.title: Caixin reveals the inside story of institutions shrinking their crypto asset businesses in Hong Kong: avoiding the systemic and dependency risks that may arise from pegging stablecoins to the US dollar.
data.description: Various institutions have been ordered to scale back their cryptoasset operations in Hong Kong. Internet platforms, Chinese brokerages, and Chinese banks, among others, have been asked to temporarily suspend all cryptoasset-related activities, including investment, trading, issuance of RWAs, and stablecoins. Brokerages licensed to provide virtual asset trading services have drawn particular scrutiny, as clients can directly trade Bitcoin, Ether, and Tether within their accounts. Hong Kong categorizes virtual assets into securities and non-securities, imposing varying degrees of restrictions on Chinese companies engaging in virtual currency-related activities, including virtual currency investment, issuance of RWAs overseas using domestic assets, provision of virtual currency trading services, and issuance of stablecoins. It is understood that the fundamental rationale behind these restrictions on investment and trading in virtual currencies by various types of institutions is to mitigate the systemic and dependency risks that may arise when domestic companies are tied to US dollar-denominated stablecoins and unanchored virtual currency systems. It is reported that the "treasury company" model will be restricted. During this wave of virtual currency innovation, the "treasury company" model, which leverages investments in cryptocurrency assets, has been rapidly replicated. Many Chinese companies listed on the Hong Kong and US stock markets have announced purchases of crypto assets such as Bitcoin and Ethereum, hoping to profit from both stock and coin prices. Now, this model is likely to be restricted. (Caixin.com)

data.title: Blockchain analytics firm Elliptic receives strategic investment from HSBC
data.description: Blockchain analytics company Elliptic announced it has received a strategic investment from HSBC. The specific investment amount has not been disclosed. It is reported that upon completion of the transaction, Richard May, Head of Financial Crime at HSBC's Corporate and Institutional Banking, will join the company's board of directors. Elliptic plans to deploy its anti-money laundering and stablecoin solutions in the traditional financial sector, helping banks assess the risk of issuer wallets before holding stablecoin reserves. (PRNewswire)

data.title: Amplify ETFs submits SOL and XRP option income ETF application to the US SEC
data.description: Amplify ETFs, a provider of exchange-traded fund solutions, announced that it has filed an application with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission for a Solana and XRP options income ETF, seeking to balance income and capital appreciation through investment in XRP, Solana price returns, and covered call options strategies. (Globenewswire)

data.title: CZ: The era of Perp DEX is coming, and high-quality projects will win in the long run
data.description: CZ wrote, "Everyone wants to build a Perp DEX now. More participants will expand the market faster, and rising tides will lift all boats. In the long run, the best builders will win. The era of Perp DEX has arrived."

data.title: Tether Appoints Benjamin Habbel as New Chief Business Officer
data.description:
According to PANews, Tether has announced the appointment of Benjamin Habbel as its new Chief Business Officer. Habbel will oversee the company's development, finance, investment, and portfolio expansion. In his role, he will focus on collaborating closely with Tether's portfolio companies and investment projects, aiming to expand the portfolio management team and empower founders and teams to achieve their visions.

Habbel brings expertise in both technology and finance. As the CEO of Limestone Capital, he established a successful alternative asset management firm, managing multiple funds with a total of $1 billion in assets and overseeing operations with over 500 employees. His career includes leadership roles at Google, founding and successfully exiting several software companies in Silicon Valley, and nearly a decade of developing Limestone into a global investment platform.

data.title: Chainlink Expands Blockchain Integration with Canton Network to Boost Institutional Adoption
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, Chainlink has announced its integration with the Canton Network, an institutional blockchain supported by global banks and technology firms, aiming to enhance the adoption of blockchain technology within institutional frameworks. This collaboration, revealed on Wednesday, incorporates Chainlink’s data services, including data streams, smartdata solutions like proof of reserve and NAVLink, and the cross-chain interoperability protocol (CCIP) into the Canton Network. Additionally, the network has joined the Chainlink Scale program, which assists in managing oracle operating costs.

Chainlink Labs will assume the role of a Canton Network “super validator” as part of the agreement. A super validator functions as a combined node, operating both a domain validator and a Canton synchronizer node, participating in the Global Synchronizer to organize and finalize cross-domain transactions, thereby ensuring consensus and interoperability across the network. Yuval Rooz, CEO of Digital Asset, the developer of the Canton Network, emphasized that Chainlink enhances governance and resilience while expanding opportunities for innovation across both traditional and decentralized finance sectors. Chainlink co-founder Sergey Nazarov expressed optimism that the partnership would facilitate large-scale real-world applications that integrate traditional and decentralized capital markets.

The Canton Network, launched in May 2023, is designed to support tokenization and large-scale financial activities, with backing from major entities such as Microsoft, Goldman Sachs, BNP Paribas, Cboe Global Markets, Digital Asset, and Paxos. The network’s governing body, previously known as the Global Synchronizer Foundation, has rebranded as the Canton Foundation, which includes prominent validators like Chainlink. The rebranding aims to better align the foundation’s purpose with the Canton Network’s objectives.

Since its inception, the Canton Network has experienced substantial growth, supported by a roster of influential partners. The network now manages over $6 trillion in on-chain real-world assets, processes $280 billion in repurchase agreements daily, and is backed by a network of 500 validators and more than 30 super validators. Recent developments include P2P.org becoming a validator on the Canton Network, and major global banks BNP Paribas and HSBC joining the Canton Foundation. This integration occurs amid a broader trend of global financial institutions accelerating blockchain initiatives, with Ripple and Securitize integrating the Ripple USD stablecoin for tokenized funds by investment firms BlackRock and VanEck, and Fnality securing $136 million from backers including Bank of America, Citi, and Temasek. Additionally, Kazakhstan’s central bank is piloting a tenge-backed stablecoin with Solana and Mastercard.

data.title: Web3+AI project SuperImage receives $5 million in strategic investment
data.description: SuperImage, a decentralized AI image generation and editing platform, announced the completion of a $5 million strategic funding round led by Genesis Block Capital. This round of financing will further drive SuperImage's product upgrades and global expansion, accelerating the adoption of decentralized AI image applications. Founded in June 2023, SuperImage currently has over one million users. The platform operates on a decentralized GPU network, aggregating idle computing power worldwide, allowing anyone to contribute their home or professional GPUs to mining and earn $SIC token rewards. Compared to centralized AI services, SuperImage's image generation costs are only one-tenth of centralized AI services, providing a low-cost, high-efficiency solution for creators, designers, and businesses. Currently, SuperImage supports three models: Hidream, Flux Dev, and Sana, meeting the needs of text generation for various styles and scenarios.

data.title: OranjeBTC purchased 3,650 BTC for $385 million, becoming the largest Bitcoin holder in Latin America
data.description: According to Cointelegraph, OranjeBTC purchased 3,650 BTC for $385 million, becoming the largest Bitcoin holder in Latin America.

data.title: Affected by CZ’s post, SFP briefly hit 0.74 USDT
data.description: CZ posted that SafePal is also in YZi Labs' portfolio, having invested in it long ago, probably shortly after investing in Trust Wallet. Influenced by this news, SFP briefly hit 0.74 USDT and is currently trading at 0.567 USDT.

data.title: Enso launches universal payment layer Checkout
data.description: Enso announced on the X platform the launch of Checkout, a universal Web 3 payment layer designed to connect CeFi and DeFi. This feature supports centralized exchange balances, fiat currencies, or any on-chain assets.

data.title: Michael Saylor predicts Bitcoin will outgrow gold 10X as corporate adoption rises
data.description:
Michael Saylor, chairman of Strategy, estimated that Bitcoin might grow ten times the size of gold as governments and corporations embrace it. He claimed that Bitcoin is programmable and borderless money, instead of the physical constraints of gold and the tariff risk. Saylor called Bitcoin the “next frontier” for national reserves and corporate treasuries. Saylor […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/michael-saylor-bitcoin-will-outgrow-gold/
